rows.question: What event caused the Liberal Party of Australia's longest-serving leader to become Prime Minister?
rows.answer:
The Liberal Party of Australia’s longest-serving leader is Robert Menzies. He first became Prime Minister in 1939 following the death of Joseph Lyons, who was leader of the United Australia Party (UAP), the predecessor to the Liberal Party.
